title: mentha longifolia var. undulata
intro: Mentha longifolia var. undulata, also known as Hoary Peppermint or Wild Peppermint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that is native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Hoary Peppermint can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
